factoring_quadratics.label: Factoring quadratics
factoring_quadratics.description: Expressing a quadratic trinomial as a product of two binomials.

distributive_property.label: Distributive property
distributive_property.description: a(b + c) = ab + ac — expanding and verifying products of a term across a sum.

sign_rules.label: Sign rules under addition
sign_rules.description: How positive and negative signs combine when adding or multiplying integers.

integer_multiplication.label: Integer multiplication
integer_multiplication.description: Multiplying positive and negative whole numbers, including sign outcomes.

integer_addition.label: Integer addition
integer_addition.description: Adding positive and negative whole numbers on a number line.

variable_notation.label: Variable notation
variable_notation.description: Reading and writing expressions like 3x, x^2, and combining like terms.

finding_factor_pairs.label: Finding factor pairs
finding_factor_pairs.description: Identifying two numbers that multiply to give a target product.

combining_like_terms.label: Combining like terms
combining_like_terms.description: Adding or subtracting terms that share the same variable and exponent.

misconception_types.label: Incomplete distribution
misconception_types.pattern: Student expands a binomial product but drops or misapplies a term, suggesting the distributive property itself is not fully internalized.

misconception_types.label: Sign inversion
misconception_types.pattern: Student finds the correct factor pair (correct magnitudes) but assigns the wrong signs to one or both factors.

misconception_types.label: Wrong factor pair
misconception_types.pattern: Student selects numbers that do not multiply to the constant term at all — the search for valid factor pairs broke down.

misconception_types.label: Like terms combined incorrectly
misconception_types.pattern: Student merges unlike terms (e.g. treats x and x^2 as the same) or miscombines coefficients.

misconception_types.label: Basic arithmetic slip
misconception_types.pattern: The method/approach is correct but a basic multiplication or addition fact is wrong.
